Class TxConflictRegistry

java.lang.Object
ca.yorku.cmg.cnsim.engine.transaction.TxConflictRegistry
public class TxConflictRegistry extends Object

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    TxConflictRegistry(int size)
    Creates a registry for conflicts between IDs 1..size.

  • Constructor Details

    • TxConflictRegistry

      public TxConflictRegistry(int size)
      Creates a registry for conflicts between IDs 1..size.

  • Method Details

    • getMatch

      public int getMatch(int id)
      Gets the partner of ID 'id'. Returns -1 if unmatched, -2 if uninitialized.

      JML Contract:

         //@ requires 1 invalid input: '<'= id invalid input: '<'= size;
   //@ ensures \result == match[id];
      Parameters:
      id - the transaction ID to query
      Returns:
      the matching transaction ID, -1 if unmatched, or -2 if uninitialized
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if id is out of range [1..size]

    • neutralize

      public void neutralize()
      Neutralizes all conflicts by setting all matches to -1. After calling this method, all IDs will be unmatched.

      JML Contract:

         //@ ensures (\forall int i; 1 invalid input: '<'= i invalid input: '<'= size; getMatch(i) == -1);

    • uninitialized

      public boolean uninitialized(int id)
      Checks if the given ID is uninitialized. An uninitialized ID has a match value of -2.

      JML Contract:

         //@ requires 1 invalid input: '<'= id invalid input: '<'= size;
   //@ ensures \result == (match[id] == -2);
      Parameters:
      id - the transaction ID to check
      Returns:
      true if the ID is uninitialized, false otherwise

    • setMatch

      public void setMatch(int a, int b)
      Creates a conflict pair between two transaction IDs. Both IDs will reference each other as conflicts. Overwrites previous matches if any.

      JML Contract:

         //@ requires 1 invalid input: '<'= a invalid input: '<'= size;
   //@ requires 1 invalid input: '<'= b invalid input: '<'= size;
   //@ requires a != b;
   //@ ensures getMatch(a) == b;
   //@ ensures getMatch(b) == a;
      Parameters:
      a - first transaction ID
      b - second transaction ID
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if a or b is out of range [1..size]
      IllegalArgumentException - if a equals b

    • noMatch

      public void noMatch(int id)
      Removes any match for the given ID. If the ID has a partner, that partner's match is also cleared. After this operation, the ID will have a match value of -1.

      JML Contract:

         //@ requires 1 invalid input: '<'= id invalid input: '<'= size;
   //@ ensures getMatch(id) == -1;
   //@ ensures (\old(getMatch(id)) > 0) ==>
   //@         getMatch((int)\old(getMatch(id))) == -1;
      Parameters:
      id - the transaction ID to clear
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if id is out of range [1..size]

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the conflict registry. The format is [(1 invalid input: '<'-> match1), (2 invalid input: '<'-> match2), ...].

      JML Contract:

         //@ ensures \result != null;
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string showing all ID-match pairs
